review:
The current crypto regime remains a confirmed bear phase with high confidence, and the composite signal held at No Action through the final week of June. There was no regime change this week - the framework produced no actionable dislocation. BTC closed near 60,000, ETH near 1,570, and SOL near 70, with Fear and Greed sitting at 18, which is deep in Extreme Fear territory. Funding rate z-scores are close to flat, suggesting neither crowded shorts nor leveraged longs are driving the tape - this is quiet, grinding deterioration rather than a capitulation event.

The one-month and one-quarter pictures are unambiguous in their persistence. Every single day of the trailing quarter has been classified as Bear, with no phase transitions observed. The action distribution over 72 days shows 67 No Action signals and only 5 Buy signals - those five buys occurred in May and did not alter the dominant regime. Average BTC scores in the low-to-mid 40s confirm the composite remains below any threshold that would generate a dislocation signal. ETH and SOL are underperforming BTC on every relevant window, with ETH down roughly 21 percent on both the one-month and quarter, signalling persistent institutional risk reduction in the altcoin complex rather than selective rotation.

Year-to-date, the crypto cycle narrative is straightforward: the post-halving reflationary impulse that was expected to carry into mid-2026 has failed to materialise. ETH/BTC z-scores averaging negative 0.84 YTD and SOL/BTC z-scores averaging negative 1.45 confirm that risk appetite has collapsed progressively down the quality curve. There is no institutional rotation signal present - ETH is not outperforming, SOL is not leading.

The cross-asset context is the notable asymmetry here. Equity markets are registering as Business As Usual - VIX near its 10-year median, credit spreads calm, no stress flags in either high yield or equity vol. SPY is down modestly on the week but not in distress. This means crypto stress is idiosyncratic, not a spillover from a broader risk-off. For the framework signal to shift, the analyst would need to see BTC composite scores recover above 55 alongside a stabilisation in ETH/BTC z-scores - neither condition is close to being met.
